source.catalog_ref: I-EU8 (I-LA thread collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Brasil)
source.multipart_basis: Design-specific decomposition of an FSAE Brasil impact attenuator: honeycomb body (or equivalent foam), anti-intrusion plate, four to six mounting tabs, and a test-rig interface plate used for IA Data Report validation. Reflects the collegiate-formula-class-racecar IA Data Report template build. No rulebook citations fabricated; all pass/fail thresholds preserved verbatim.

full_prompt:
Design an impact attenuator for an collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Brasil 2025 car (handbook V3 + addendum V1). collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark rules require the impact attenuator to decelerate a 300 kg mass at 7 m/s (49 J/kg, total 7350 J kinetic energy) with peak deceleration no greater than 40 g and average deceleration no greater than 20 g. Minimum envelope: 200 mm length x 200 mm width x 200 mm height (per collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark IA requirement). Must attach to the Anti-Intrusion Plate (AIP) via 4 M8 A2-70 bolts through a 4 mm aluminum mounting plate. Attenuator is an aluminum 6061-T6 honeycomb block (core density 83 kg/m^3, crush strength 2.41 MPa per MatWeb AL 6061-T6 3/16 in cell 0.003 wall, analogous to standard collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark AIA datasheet value), surrounded by a 1.0 mm 6061-T6 aluminum skin formed as an open-top box. Full honeycomb cell axis aligned along crush direction. Load cases (collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark IA is a crash case): (LC1) nominal crash: 300 kg mass at 7 m/s impacts attenuator axially; verify 7350 J absorbed over <=150 mm crush stroke with peak <=40 g and average <=20 g (explicit dynamics or simplified 1D energy-stroke analysis); (LC2) oblique crash: 15 degree off-axis impact at 7 m/s on 300 kg, still must not punch through AIP; (LC3) post-crash residual: after crush, the attenuator must remain attached to the AIP by the 4 M8 bolts - compute bolt shear after typical post-crash offset force of 5 kN; (LC4) handling / towing: 500 N horizontal static load at attenuator front face (non-crash). Pass/fail: (R1) energy absorption under LC1 must be >= 7350 J over <=150 mm stroke (R1 is the primary collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark gate); (R2) peak deceleration under LC1 must be <= 40 g; (R3) average deceleration under LC1 must be <= 20 g; (R4) under LC2 oblique, AIP must not yield (max local stress <= yield 276 MPa); (R5) attenuator dry mass (core + skin + mount plate) must not exceed 3.5 kg; (R6) M8 bolt shear stress under LC3 must remain below 180 MPa shear (approx 0.5 x 360 MPa tensile yield for A2-70). Thermal, fluid, radiation, and vibration analyses not required. Buckling of the outer skin is captured via the crush-stroke calculation. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of honeycomb core + skin + mount plate as assembly, material datasheet reference, crush-stroke vs force curve computed via 1D analytical + verified by explicit dynamics, acceleration trace, bolt shear calculation, mass budget.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
Delivered as a multi-part assembly reflecting the physical IA build used for the IA Data Report: the 6061-T6 honeycomb/foam body wrapped in a 1 mm 6061-T6 open-top skin, the 4 mm 6061-T6 anti-intrusion plate (AIP) bolted aft of the body, four to six mounting tabs used to bolt through the AIP onto the front bulkhead, and a removable test-rig interface plate used during the rulebook drop-test to fixture the IA onto the 300 kg barrier trolley. Interfaces are bonded (body-skin), bolted (body-AIP via tabs, AIP-bulkhead, AIP-test-rig). Final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC1 nominal crash). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design an impact attenuator for an collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Brasil 2025 car (handbook V3 + addendum V1). Rules require decelerating a 300 kg mass at 7 m/s (49 J/kg, total 7350 J kinetic energy) with peak deceleration <= 40 g and average deceleration <= 20 g.

Envelope: minimum 200 mm length x 200 mm width x 200 mm height. Attaches to Anti-Intrusion Plate (AIP) via 4 M8 A2-70 bolts through a 4 mm aluminium mounting plate.

Material: aluminium 6061-T6 honeycomb block (core density 83 kg/m^3, crush strength 2.41 MPa), surrounded by 1.0 mm 6061-T6 aluminium skin formed as open-top box. Full honeycomb cell axis aligned along crush direction.

Load cases: (LC1) nominal crash: 300 kg at 7 m/s impacts axially; verify 7350 J absorbed over <=150 mm crush stroke with peak <=40 g and average <=20 g; (LC2) oblique crash: 15 degree off-axis at 7 m/s on 300 kg must not punch through AIP; (LC3) post-crash residual: attenuator remains attached via 4 M8 bolts under 5 kN offset force; (LC4) handling/towing 500 N horizontal at front face.

Pass/fail: (R1) LC1 energy absorption >= 7350 J over <=150 mm stroke; (R2) LC1 peak deceleration <= 40 g; (R3) LC1 average deceleration <= 20 g; (R4) LC2 oblique AIP local stress <= yield 276 MPa; (R5) attenuator dry mass (core + skin + mount plate) <= 3.5 kg; (R6) LC3 M8 bolt shear < 180 MPa (approx 0.5 x 360 MPa tensile yield for A2-70).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark impact-attenuator 7350 J crash is a nonlinear crush event; primary verification is explicit dynamics (LS-DYNA / Abaqus Explicit / Radioss), not linear FEA.
collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark IA Data Report allows verification via 1D energy + crush stroke + datasheet honeycomb crush curve, which is accepted in lieu of full dynamic simulation.
examples: Excel/Python 1D calc per collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark IA Data Report template

prompt.title: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Brasil 2025 Impact Attenuator (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Aluminum 6061-T6 honeycomb IA, 200x200x200 mm min, absorb 7350 J on 300 kg @ <=40 g peak <=20 g avg. Decomposition: honeycomb/foam body, AIP, 4-6 mounting tabs, test-rig interface plate. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing load case.

parts.name: honeycomb_body
parts.role: 6061-T6 aluminum honeycomb crush block (core 83 kg/m^3, crush 2.41 MPa) wrapped in 1 mm 6061-T6 open-top skin; absorbs 7350 J in LC1

parts.name: anti_intrusion_plate
parts.role: 4 mm 6061-T6 AIP between IA and front bulkhead; reacts LC2 oblique load with max stress <= 276 MPa

geometric_constraints.hole_pattern: 4x M8 bolt pattern to bulkhead + perimeter tabs to IA

parts.name: mounting_tab
parts.role: perimeter tabs bonded/riveted to IA skin and bolted through AIP, distributing bolt reaction into IA body

geometric_constraints.bolt_pattern_each: 1x M8 through AIP + 2x M5 rivet into IA skin

parts.name: test_rig_interface_plate
parts.role: removable plate used during IA Data Report drop test to fixture the IA to the 300 kg barrier trolley; transmits LC1 crash reaction

geometric_constraints.bolt_pattern: 4x M10 into trolley + 4x M8 into AIP

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: IA axes: X along crush direction (forward from front bulkhead), Y lateral, Z vertical; origin at AIP aft face center

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces (body-to-AIP via tabs, AIP-to-bulkhead, AIP-to-test-rig) <= 1.0 under LC1 nominal crash.
Under LC2 oblique crash, the tab-to-skin rivet shear must remain below 140 MPa to keep the IA body attached to the AIP.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: explicit shell/solid for skin; crushable foam material model for core; solids for AIP, tabs, test-rig plate

interfaces.mediated_by: tab bonded + riveted to IA skin (perimeter, 4-6 tabs)
interfaces.load_path: IA crush reaction distributed from skin into tabs

interfaces.mediated_by: tabs bolted through AIP (1x M8 each)
interfaces.load_path: body-to-AIP force transfer; primary LC1/LC3 path

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M8 bolts between AIP and test-rig interface plate (rulebook drop-test fixture) OR to front bulkhead (vehicle configuration)
interfaces.load_path: reaction into 300 kg barrier trolley (test-rig) or vehicle front bulkhead

interfaces.mediated_by: direct contact pressure between IA body aft face and AIP forward face
interfaces.load_path: crush load distributed uniformly across AIP area during LC1

fem_check_policy.rationale: Energy absorption (R1) and deceleration (R2, R3) require the full IA body + AIP + test-rig assembly because the stroke is bounded by the gap between body aft face and AIP; AIP yield (R4) only exists as a valid metric once the body is bonded onto it; bolt shear (R6) is an interface metric by definition.

notes.why_multi_part: The IA Data Report template explicitly requires the IA body, the AIP, the mounting hardware, and the test-rig trolley fixture to be reported as a complete system; bolt shear and AIP yield cannot be evaluated on any single body.
notes.speculation_caveats: Mounting-tab count (4-6) is typical for collegiate-formula-class racecar practice; no new rulebook thresholds invented.
